mechanism_of_action: Octapeptin inhibits the respiratory-linked generation of membrane potentials in E. coli membrane vesicles, leading to inhibition of leucine transport. It disrupts the selective ion permeability of the cytoplasmic membrane by increasing permeability to protons and potassium, thereby relaxing the membrane potential. Proposed mechanism involves electrostatic interaction with membrane lipid phosphates and insertion of a fatty acid tail into the membrane, causing disruption of lipid packing and increased small ion permeability.
